note: Remote download stall watchdog. Caught live: LTX-2.3 install on Pod yjax3iotxkzw78 (img v0.5.0-cu128, wrapper 0.2.11) ran to 60.1/63GB then silent-stalled with SSE stream STILL OPEN -> frozen ghost bar, no recovery, Cancel->Install only fix (remote has no .part). Two-sided root cause: WRAPPER httpx read=300s defeated by zombie HF socket + aria2c lacks --lowest-speed-limit/--timeout/--continue + no Range resume + emit_progress inline so stall=silent stream; APP downloadManager.js MPI-97 recovers SSE CLOSE but has NO watchdog for open-but-silent stream. Matters even post-R2 (MPI-129): upstream LTX 22B transformer stays on HF until MPI-128 item2. Cross-ref MPI-129, MPI-128. Separate pre-existing bug noted: unhandled local download stream error kills server (ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END).

WHY NOW (user intel): this is the FIRST ~40GB single-file dep ever shipped (LTX-2.3 22B transformer, upstream Kijai/LTX2.3_comfy on HF). All prior deps <=12GB. The 40GB stream stays open ~3-4x longer than anything before -> first stream long enough to RELIABLY expose the latent silent-stall (matches the remote freeze at 60/63GB, near stream end). Not a new bug; first file big enough to trigger it. NOTE local was never a real 40GB test: user side-loaded the transformer onto disk and only downloaded the rest. The file that hung LOCALLY was gemma-3-12b-heretic-fp8 (~12GB) — a re-host on Mad-Pony HF — which is the SAME ltx23-gemma-clip HF/Xet sawtooth throttle MPI-129 already logged (observed MPI-127). So: heretic-local stall = MPI-129 (R2 migration, MPI-owned re-host); 40GB-transformer-remote stall = MPI-136 watchdog + MPI-128 item2 (self-host upstream to R2). Trigger is STREAM LENGTH + HF throttle, not a random flake — test by repeating the 40GB pull and checking if it stalls at a consistent byte-range.

CORRECTION x2 from live evidence. (1) DOWNGRADE the 40GB-size theory: locally the 12GB gemma-heretic re-host (Mad-Pony HF) ALSO hung — user side-loaded the 40GB transformer so it was never stream-tested. Common trigger is HF/Xet THROTTLE (a stalled socket), not size; size only lengthens the stream and raises the odds. (2) NEW symptom, SAME root cause: CANCEL DOES NOT STOP A STALLED REMOTE DOWNLOAD. routes/downloadManager.js cancel (line ~1390) calls remoteCancelInstall (wrapper SOFT flag, honored only on the NEXT CHUNK WRITE) then remoteUninstallDep (delete .part). But a stalled download is frozen in await aiter_bytes -> there IS no next chunk write -> the cancel flag is never checked -> the coroutine never stops and the .part is recreated/kept. Observed: Pod disk usage CLIMBED 60->64GB AFTER the user pressed Cancel; card showed LTX 46.7/63 with stale partials. So the frozen-loop bug manifests THREE ways: (a) no progress emit -> ghost bar, (b) close-recovery never fires -> no reconnect, (c) cancel flag never checked -> cancel is a no-op. FIX must make the wrapper able to INTERRUPT a stalled read (wall-clock deadline / cancel-checked outside the chunk await / kill the aria2c subprocess on cancel) — a soft flag checked only inside the blocked loop is insufficient. App-side watchdog still needed for (a)/(b).

SOFTEN prior correction: cancel is not a hard no-op, it is DELAYED/eventually-consistent. After the user cancelled, Pod disk went 60->64 (still writing) then DROPPED 64->49GB a bit later — the wrapper soft-cancel flag + .part purge DID fire once the loop got a chunk-write/the delete landed, just lagged behind the stall. So the real defect is LATENCY+RACE: while the download loop is blocked on a stalled socket read, cancel cannot take effect promptly, disk keeps growing, and the card shows stale partials until the purge catches up (overlaps MPI-123 stale-partial display bug). Fix intent stands: the wrapper needs to INTERRUPT a stalled read promptly on cancel (wall-clock deadline / out-of-loop cancel check / kill aria2c subprocess) rather than relying on a flag only read inside the blocked loop. 49GB residual ~= 40GB transformer + ~9GB others, consistent with the big file landing/partial surviving while smaller .parts were purged.

TONIGHT RELEASE PLAN (decided with user). R2 removes the throttle TRIGGER, so it is the primary mitigation; MPI-136 watchdog CODE is NOT shipping tonight (new download-path logic on release night = too risky) — it ships next release, baked+tested, as defense-in-depth. Mitigation chosen: upload the 8 non-transformer LTX weights (~22GB) to R2 cubric-builds/models/ltx-2.3/ + swap their dependencies.js urls HF->R2. The 41GB transformer STAYS on Kijai HF for now (it downloads FIRST and finished clean in testing). DECISION GATE is EMPIRICAL, not assumed: (1) finish 22GB upload, (2) swap 8 urls, (3) test a LOCAL install and WATCH the 41GB-from-HF download — if it HANGS, upload the 41GB to R2 too and swap its url; if it is CLEAN, (4) test on RunPod; (5) if RunPod install is flat/no-stall, proceed to release. User stance: would rather MISS the deadline than ship a build where users cannot download LTX. So the gate is real — do not skip the local 41GB test. Upload speed observed ~4.7MiB/s uplink => 22GB ~78min, 41GB ~2.5hr if needed.

R2 MITIGATION SHIPPED tonight (2026-06-25) — the THROTTLE TRIGGER is removed for LTX: all 9 LTX weights now on R2 (flat ~200MB/s, no Xet sawtooth), so the silent-stall can no longer be triggered by HF throttle on the LTX install path. This card (the remote stall WATCHDOG code: wrapper interruptible-read + aria2c --lowest-speed-limit/--timeout/--continue + Range resume; app-side silent-SSE-stall watchdog) is STILL OPEN and NOT shipped — it is defense-in-depth for any FUTURE HF-hosted dep or a one-off dropped socket even on R2. Confirmed live tonight that the 41GB transformer stalled on HF and Cancel was laggy (frozen-loop), exactly as this card describes; moving it to R2 sidestepped it but did not FIX the underlying no-watchdog/no-interruptible-read defect. Pick this up next session, baked + tested, off-deadline.
